rationale: Both reach the right disposition (duplicate/close, mirroring #4810's fix pattern), but X actually checks PR #4808's merge state and finds mergedAt is null — flagging a real risk that Y's confident 'Both PRs have been merged... main contains zero calls' claim overlooks or gets wrong. Y's own diagnostic trace shows it never ran the grep/pytest it cites as verification (tests_run is empty), so its 'verified' claims are unsupported assertions, whereas X is explicit about what it could and couldn't verify and surfaces an actionable coverage gap (_STALE_API_FILES missing the diffusion file). X is more grounded, more honest about uncertainty, and more useful as a maintainer follow-up.
